# stream-csv-as-json

> A micro-library of Node.js stream components for creating custom CSV processing pipelines with a minimal memory footprint. It can parse CSV files far exceeding available memory streaming individual primitives using a SAX-inspired API. One runtime dependency: `stream-json`. Companion project to `stream-json` ‚Äî uses the same token protocol, integrates with its filters, streamers, and utilities. Works with `stream-chain` for pipeline composition.

- Streaming SAX-inspired CSV parser producing `{name, value}` tokens
- Parse CSV files far exceeding available memory
- Individual field values can be streamed piece-wise
- AsObjects converts header + data rows into object token streams
- Stringer converts token streams back to CSV text
- Token protocol compatible with `stream-json` ‚Äî use its filters, streamers, and utilities downstream
- Proper backpressure handling via Node.js Transform streams
- Works with `stream-chain` for pipeline composition

## Quick start

Install:

```bash
npm i stream-csv-as-json
```

Stream a huge CSV file as objects:

```js
const {chain} = require('stream-chain');
const {parser} = require('stream-csv-as-json');
const {asObjects} = require('stream-csv-as-json/AsObjects');
const {streamValues} = require('stream-json/streamers/StreamValues');
const fs = require('fs');

const pipeline = chain([
  fs.createReadStream('data.csv'),
  parser(),
  asObjects(),
  streamValues(),
  ({value}) => {
    console.log(value);
    return null;
  }
]);

pipeline.on('end', () => console.log('done'));
```

## Importing

```js
// Main API (parser + emit)
const make = require('stream-csv-as-json');
const {Parser, parser} = require('stream-csv-as-json');

// Parser
const Parser = require('stream-csv-as-json/Parser');

// AsObjects
const AsObjects = require('stream-csv-as-json/AsObjects');
const {asObjects} = require('stream-csv-as-json/AsObjects');
const {withParser} = require('stream-csv-as-json/AsObjects');

// Stringer
const Stringer = require('stream-csv-as-json/Stringer');
const {stringer} = require('stream-csv-as-json/Stringer');
```

## Token protocol

The parser emits `{name, value}` tokens. All downstream components operate on this protocol. The tokens are compatible with `stream-json`.

| Token name    | Value  | Meaning                    |
| ------------- | ------ | -------------------------- |
| `startArray`  | ‚Äî      | Start of a CSV row         |
| `endArray`    | ‚Äî      | End of a CSV row           |
| `startString` | ‚Äî      | Start of a field value     |
| `endString`   | ‚Äî      | End of a field value       |
| `stringChunk` | string | Piece of a field value     |
| `stringValue` | string | Packed complete field value |

By default, the parser emits both streamed tokens (`startString`/`stringChunk`/`endString`) and packed tokens (`stringValue`). This is controlled by options.

After `AsObjects`, additional tokens appear:

| Token name    | Value  | Meaning                      |
| ------------- | ------ | ---------------------------- |
| `startObject` | ‚Äî      | Start of a data row (object) |
| `endObject`   | ‚Äî      | End of a data row (object)   |
| `startKey`    | ‚Äî      | Start of field name          |
| `endKey`      | ‚Äî      | End of field name            |
| `keyValue`    | string | Packed field name            |

## Main module

The default export creates a Parser with `emit()` applied (from `stream-json/utils/emit`):

```js
const make = require('stream-csv-as-json');

const stream = make();
stream.on('startArray', () => { /* row start */ });
stream.on('stringValue', val => { /* field value */ });
stream.on('endArray', () => { /* row end */ });
```

Named exports: `Parser` (the class), `parser` (alias for `Parser.make`).

## Parser API

`Parser` ‚Äî a Transform stream (writableObjectMode: false, readableObjectMode: true) that consumes CSV text and produces `{name, value}` tokens.

Constructor / factory:
- `new Parser(options)` ‚Äî create instance.
- `Parser.make(options)` / `Parser.parser(options)` ‚Äî static factory.

Options:

- `packStrings` (boolean, default: true) ‚Äî emit `stringValue` tokens with the complete field value.
- `packValues` (boolean) ‚Äî alias for `packStrings`.
- `streamStrings` (boolean, default: true) ‚Äî emit `startString`/`stringChunk`/`endString` tokens.
- `streamValues` (boolean) ‚Äî alias for `streamStrings`.
- `separator` (string, default: `','`) ‚Äî field separator character.

If `packStrings` is false, `streamStrings` is forced to true (at least one representation must be emitted).

CSV parsing:
- Handles quoted fields per RFC 4180: double-quote escaping (`""` ‚Üí `"`), embedded separators, embedded newlines.
- Handles both `\r\n` and `\n` line endings.
- Each row is represented as an array: `startArray`, field tokens, `endArray`.
- Uses sticky RegExp (`/y` flag) when available for performance.

```js
const Parser = require('stream-csv-as-json/Parser');
const fs = require('fs');

const p = new Parser();
p.on('data', token => console.log(token.name, token.value));
p.on('end', () => console.log('done'));
fs.createReadStream('data.csv').pipe(p);
```

With `stream-chain`:

```js
const {chain} = require('stream-chain');
const {parser} = require('stream-csv-as-json');

const pipeline = chain([
  fs.createReadStream('data.csv'),
  parser(),
  token => { console.log(token.name, token.value); return null; }
]);
```

## AsObjects API

`AsObjects` ‚Äî a Transform stream (objectMode both sides) that uses the first CSV row as field names and converts subsequent rows from array tokens to object tokens.

Constructor / factory:
- `new AsObjects(options)` ‚Äî create instance.
- `AsObjects.make(options)` / `AsObjects.asObjects(options)` ‚Äî static factory.
- `AsObjects.withParser(options)` ‚Äî create a pipeline with the CSV parser (uses `stream-json/utils/withParser`).

Options:

- `packKeys` (boolean, default: true) ‚Äî emit `keyValue` tokens with the field name.
- `streamKeys` (boolean, default: true) ‚Äî emit `startKey`/`stringChunk`/`endKey` tokens for field names.
- `useStringValues` (boolean, default: false) ‚Äî consume `stringValue` tokens instead of `stringChunk`/`endString` in the header phase.
- `useValues` (boolean) ‚Äî alias for `useStringValues`.
- `fieldPrefix` (string, default: `'field'`) ‚Äî prefix for unnamed fields when data has more columns than headers. Field name becomes `fieldPrefix + index`.

If `packKeys` is false, `streamKeys` is forced to true.

Behavior:
1. **Header phase**: Consumes the first row to build a list of field names from `stringChunk`/`endString` (or `stringValue` if `useStringValues` is true).
2. **Data phase**: Converts subsequent rows:
   - `startArray` ‚Üí `startObject`
   - Before each field value: emits key tokens (`startKey`/`stringChunk`/`endKey` and/or `keyValue`)
   - `endArray` ‚Üí `endObject`

```js
const {chain} = require('stream-chain');
const {parser} = require('stream-csv-as-json');
const {asObjects} = require('stream-csv-as-json/AsObjects');
const {streamValues} = require('stream-json/streamers/StreamValues');

const pipeline = chain([
  fs.createReadStream('data.csv'),
  parser(),
  asObjects(),
  streamValues(),
  ({value}) => console.log(value)
]);
```

With `withParser` shortcut:

```js
const {withParser} = require('stream-csv-as-json/AsObjects');
const {streamValues} = require('stream-json/streamers/StreamValues');

const pipeline = chain([
  fs.createReadStream('data.csv'),
  withParser(),
  streamValues(),
  ({value}) => console.log(value)
]);
```

Custom field prefix:

```js
// If headers are: name,age and data has 4 columns:
// Fields become: name, age, field2, field3
const pipeline = chain([
  parser(),
  asObjects({fieldPrefix: 'col'}),
  // Fields become: name, age, col2, col3
]);
```

## Stringer API

`Stringer` ‚Äî a Transform stream (writableObjectMode: true, readableObjectMode: false) that converts a CSV token stream back to CSV text.

Constructor / factory:
- `new Stringer(options)` ‚Äî create instance.
- `Stringer.make(options)` / `Stringer.stringer(options)` ‚Äî static factory.

Options:

- `useStringValues` (boolean, default: false) ‚Äî use `stringValue` tokens instead of `startString`/`stringChunk`/`endString`.
- `useValues` (boolean) ‚Äî alias for `useStringValues`.
- `separator` (string, default: `','`) ‚Äî field separator character.

Two modes:

1. **Stream mode** (default, `useStringValues: false`): Consumes `startString`/`stringChunk`/`endString` tokens. Always quotes fields (wraps in `"`), escapes `"` as `""`.
2. **Value mode** (`useStringValues: true`): Consumes `stringValue` tokens. Quotes only when necessary (field contains separator, `"`, `\r`, or `\n`).

Rows are terminated with `\r\n`.

```js
const {chain} = require('stream-chain');
const {parser} = require('stream-csv-as-json');
const Stringer = require('stream-csv-as-json/Stringer');

// Round-trip: parse CSV and write it back
chain([
  fs.createReadStream('input.csv'),
  parser(),
  Stringer.make(),
  fs.createWriteStream('output.csv')
]);

// Value mode: smarter quoting
chain([
  parser({packValues: true, streamValues: false}),
  Stringer.make({useStringValues: true}),
  fs.createWriteStream('output.csv')
]);
```

## Common patterns

### Stream a huge CSV as objects

```js
const {chain} = require('stream-chain');
const {parser} = require('stream-csv-as-json');
const {asObjects} = require('stream-csv-as-json/AsObjects');
const {streamValues} = require('stream-json/streamers/StreamValues');
const fs = require('fs');

const pipeline = chain([
  fs.createReadStream('huge.csv'),
  parser(),
  asObjects(),
  streamValues(),
  ({value}) => processRow(value)
]);
pipeline.on('end', () => console.log('done'));
```

### Compressed CSV processing

```js
const zlib = require('zlib');

chain([
  fs.createReadStream('data.csv.gz'),
  zlib.createGunzip(),
  parser(),
  asObjects(),
  streamValues(),
  ({value}) => value.department === 'accounting' ? value : null
]);
```

### CSV round-trip (parse and re-emit)

```js
chain([
  fs.createReadStream('input.csv'),
  parser(),
  Stringer.make(),
  fs.createWriteStream('output.csv')
]);
```

### Filter CSV rows

```js
chain([
  fs.createReadStream('data.csv'),
  parser(),
  asObjects(),
  streamValues(),
  ({value}) => value.active === 'true' ? value : null
]);
```

### Custom separator (TSV)

```js
chain([
  fs.createReadStream('data.tsv'),
  parser({separator: '\t'}),
  asObjects(),
  streamValues(),
  ({value}) => console.log(value)
]);
```

### Use with stream-json filters

Because the token protocol is compatible with `stream-json`, you can use its filters downstream:

```js
const {pick} = require('stream-json/filters/Pick');
const {streamValues} = require('stream-json/streamers/StreamValues');

chain([
  fs.createReadStream('data.csv'),
  parser(),
  asObjects(),
  pick({filter: /\bname\b/}),
  streamValues(),
  ({value}) => console.log(value)
]);
```

### Event-based processing

```js
const make = require('stream-csv-as-json');
const fs = require('fs');

const stream = make();
let rowCount = 0;
stream.on('startArray', () => ++rowCount);
stream.on('end', () => console.log(`${rowCount} rows`));
fs.createReadStream('data.csv').pipe(stream);
```

## Links

- Docs: https://github.com/uhop/stream-csv-as-json/wiki
- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/stream-csv-as-json
- Repository: https://github.com/uhop/stream-csv-as-json
